steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage moves from Shakespeare being recognized by 1593 to him delighting audiences in 1599. The two events are simply separated in time — the second is not a restatement, contrast, or result of the first. The blank needs a transition that signals forward movement in time.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — In other words,: Signals restatement of the same idea. The 1599 play is a new development, not a rephrasing of the 1593 recognition. ✗
  • Choice B — Conversely,: Signals direct opposition or contrast. Both sentences describe career achievements moving in the same positive direction. ✗
  • Choice C — Thus,: Signals logical consequence. The 1599 play is not caused by the 1593 recognition — it is simply a later event. ✗
  • Choice D — Later,: Signals temporal progression from an earlier point to a later one, which is exactly the relationship between 1593 and 1599. ✓
D is correct.
